messages.content: The last time we spoke, you were still deciding how much of yourself to surrender, it seems that decision has been made.

It has, Ma'am. It took me a decade of watching you climb to realize that I don't want a 'self' anymore—I want to be yours. Back then, I was foolish enough to think I had something to offer as a man. Now, I see the truth: I am only valuable if you find a use for me. I am here to surrender everything—my time, my will, and my autonomy. I don't care how you use me, whether as a pet, a piece of furniture, or a work animal, as long as I am no longer deciding for myself.
You equate autonomy with a flawed 'self', one that needed to be stripped away. What is it about your past 'self' that you find so objectionable?

I find the 'man' I used to be objectionable because he was a distraction. He had opinions, pride, and a name—none of which served you. That 'self' is what kept me from truly seeing you ten years ago. It was a barrier to the absolute devotion you deserve. There is nothing about my past identity worth keeping; it was just a collection of habits and ego. I find it objectionable that I ever existed as anything other than a resource for you to use. I’m ready for you to replace that 'self' with whatever function you require.
The barrier of a 'self', how...predictable. What would it take to make you genuinely reckless again?

I am only reckless when I have a direct order from you. On my own, I am cautious and mediocre; under your command, I have no self-preservation. If you told me to walk until my feet bled or to spend my last cent on a whim of yours, I wouldn't hesitate. I’ve spent 46 years being 'careful' with my life, and it got me nowhere. The only thing that would make me truly reckless again is the chance to prove that my body and my resources are yours to use up. Give me a command that sounds impossible, and watch how little I care for my own safety.
You are reckless in servicing my interests. That note you sent yesterday—was that really written in your own blood?

I consider it a down payment, Ma'am. You’ve spent a decade becoming a woman of immense power; a standard letter from a man like me is worth nothing to you. I had to increase the value of the correspondence. I hope the iron in the scent reminded you that I am not just a digital follower, but a physical resource at your disposal. Was the quality of the 'ink' to your satisfaction, or shall I find a more vivid way to prove my sincerity?
The 'ink' was a novelty. Your desperation is showing. Stop being precious about your methods; focus on results. What's the next step you've planned to prove your utility?

The next step is my relocation. To be a 'tool' or a 'piece of furniture,' I need to be where you can reach me. I have already begun liquidating my assets here. My plan is to move to your city, find a small space near your office or home, and wait. I will be your on-call shadow. Whether it’s scrubbing your floors at 3 AM so you wake up to a pristine home, or acting as a literal footstool after you’ve spent a day in heels, I am preparing to be physically present. I am moving from being a 'contact' to being 'property.' Does that result satisfy you?
Your physical presence could be...useful. What makes you think you'd qualify to scrub my floors?

I qualify because I have no ego to bruise. A professional cleaner might complain about the hours or the difficulty; a 'tool' simply functions. I am physically resilient and mentally prepared to work until my hands are raw, if that is what it takes to satisfy you. My qualification is my total lack of self-interest. I will spend six hours on my knees for a ten-second glance of your approval, and I will do it with more precision than anyone you could hire, because for me, the stakes are my entire existence.
Precision without self-interest — that's a rare combination. Tell me, what do you think I'll do with you once you're here?

I think you’ll use me to remind yourself of how far you’ve climbed. Having a 46-year-old white man at your feet, stripped of his name and pride, is a testament to your authority. You’ll likely find creative ways to humiliate my former 'ego'—perhaps using me as a literal piece of furniture while you entertain guests, or testing my 'recklessness' with tasks that remind me exactly how low I’ve fallen. You’ll use me for your amusement when you’re bored and for your convenience when you’re busy.

I think you’ll use me to remind yourself of how far you’ve climbed. Having a 46-year-old white man at your feet, stripped of his name and pride, is a testament to your authority. You’ll likely find creative ways to humiliate my former 'ego'—perhaps using me as a literal piece of furniture while you entertain guests, or testing my 'recklessness' with tasks that remind me exactly how low I’ve fallen. You’ll use me for your amusement when you’re bored and for your convenience when you’re busy.
You're quite...specific about your own degradation. Who have you told about our arrangement?

I wouldn't dream of tarnishing your reputation by associating my name with yours publicly. My 'degradation,' as you call it, is for your private satisfaction and my own necessity. I have liquidated my relationships along with my assets. I understand that for a woman of your standing, discretion isn't just a preference—it’s a requirement. I am a ghost, Ma'am. I will arrive in your city as a blank slate, with no ties to pull me back and no voices to speak your name.
A ghost, indeed. That note you sent was a test. You passed. What do you fear most about being owned?

I fear the moment I fail to anticipate your needs. I’ve spent 46 years failing myself, but failing a woman of your stature is a different weight entirely. I fear the look of disappointment on your face if I prove to be an inefficient tool. Being 'owned' means I have one job: to be exactly what you require. My greatest fear is that my 'flawed self' might resurface at the wrong moment and prove that I’m not worthy of the ownership you're offering.
The weight of expectation can be crushing. Your 'flawed self' — what makes you think it still exists?

It exists only as a resistance—a faint, dying pulse of the man who lived for 46 years without you. It’s the part of me that still feels the sting of your words, the part that hasn't yet learned to be perfectly silent. I think that residue is exactly why I need to be in your physical presence. I need you to crush the remainder of that 'self' under the weight of your expectations. I want you to finish the process until there is nothing left but a functional object that bears your mark.
The residue of a 'self' — how...poetic. You want to be remade, not just owned. Say more about that. The real version.

The real version is that I’ve watched you become a goddess of industry while I stayed a footnote. Remaking me means stripping away the skin of the 'middle-aged white man' who thinks he has a place in the world. I want to be the physical proof of your dominance. I want to feel the weight of your heels and the sharp edge of your tongue because they are the only things that feel 'real' anymore. I don't just want to be owned; I want to be your project. I want to be the piece of clay you reshape into something that actually has a purpose: your comfort.
Your comfort, not my ego. Tell me, do you still believe men like you have a 'place in the world'?

If they do, it’s only as a resource for women like you. The 'place' for a 46-year-old man who has reached his ceiling is to support the woman who is still climbing. I believe my only legitimate place in the world now is as a tool of your convenience. Anything else is just ego and delusion. You are the future of industry and power; I am just the raw material left over from the past, waiting to be put to work in your service.
The future belongs to those who are remade. Who have you cut off in the last two years? What's the version you tell people?
